ab: omiki elreelreli vaaho omiidhe, kama ahakahimoho omece ’ahakikidha.
ch: 我有一個同學叫Vaaho，他常常忘記帶東西。
en: I have a classmate named Vaaho, he often forgets to bring things.

ab: lo solatedha, mani ’ipasolriya elreelreedha inpice, ’acocaocao la tapikikiyae solate.
ch: 上課的時候，他跟同學借鉛筆、橡皮擦或是筆記本。
en: During class, he borrowed pencils, erasers or notebooks from his classmates.

ch: 一次、兩次、三次，大家會借東西給他。
en: Once, twice, three times, everyone would lend him something.

ab: oha’eve la omiki takatakolraedha ta to’inilrao, lo ’ipasolriyadha ka olriho’okadha ka ’oilri.
ch: 但是他有個壞習慣，經常借了東西卻不還。
en: But he has a bad habit of often borrowing things but not returning them.

ch: 最後大家都不想借他東西了。
en: In the end, no one wanted to borrow anything from him.

ab: iiki ta kaeaea menane, cekelaenai molatadhe ’oliliyo.
ch: 有一天，我們大家一起去郊遊。
en: One day, we all went on an outing together.

ab: tavalrevalre’anae, vaaho ’api otavilavila’ae, la kahimoho omece oisengce.
ch: 路途中，Vaaho想上廁所，但他忘記帶衛生紙。
en: On the way, Kacaw wanted to go to the bathroom, but he forgot to bring toilet paper.

ab: mani ’ipasolriya elreelreedha oisengce, la cekelaenai miidhe ’ina, oameecekali.
ch: 他跟同學借衛生紙，可是大家都回他說，我也沒有帶耶。
en: He borrowed toilet paper from his classmates, but everyone told him that I didn’t bring them either.

ab: vaaho pokoali iya tovi. mani kasekelre miya ’ina, kani cekelaaenomi, kipasolriiyae oisengce!!
ch: Vaaho急的哭了出來。他生氣的說，為什麼大家都不借我衛生紙!!
en: Vaaho burst into tears. He said angrily, why don’t everyone lend me toilet paper!

ab: elreelreedha mani pato’idhe, olriho’oka’o ka ’iniyamece, lo pasolriyanaimiya’e kilriho’ongamo’o ka ’oilri!
ch: 同學告訴他，因為你都不自己帶，而且借給你的東西，你都不會還!
en: The classmate told him, because you didn’t bring it yourself, and you wouldn’t return the things lent to you!

ab: ’idhanaenga aoadha gako, vaaho ’anolake’enga moa gako, vaaho ’ipasolriyaedha elreelredha mani poelrenga ’oilri voa’i elreelreedha.
ch: 隔天上學，Vaaho很早就到學校了，Vaaho把跟同學借的東西都還給同學們。
en: The next day at school, Vaaho arrived at school early and returned everything he had borrowed from his classmates.

ab: vaaho mani liicinganomi iya elreelreedha, matalaki mani iya ’ina lo vilrilaenga amo ’iniyamecengalrao ’ahakikili, icongonganomi elreelreeli ’amadhalae dholronomi pasolriyae ’ahakiki.
ch: Vaaho向同學們道歉，並說他以後一定會自己帶東西，也謝謝同學們之前願意借他東西。
en: Vaaho apologized to the students and said that he would definitely bring his own things in the future, and thanked the classmates for their willingness to lend him things before.
